title: setuptools build_clib command
content:
This module overrides the default `distutils.command.build_clib` behavior to add a rudimentary timestamp-based dependency system and support for more keys in the `build_info` dictionary.

### Purpose

The primary goal of this override is to prevent recompilation of C/C++ libraries if their source files or specified dependencies haven't changed, and to provide a more flexible way to define compilation flags and object dependencies.

### Key Features

-   **Timestamp-based Dependency Checking**: Implements a system to check if source files or their dependencies have been modified since the last build, thus avoiding unnecessary recompilation.
-   **Extended `build_info`**: Enhances the `build_info` dictionary with new keys:
    -   `obj_deps`: A dictionary mapping source filenames to a list of their specific dependencies. An empty string key (`''`) is used for global dependencies applicable to all sources.
    -   `cflags`: A list of additional flags to pass to the compiler.

### Usage Example

When defining libraries in your `setup.py` or equivalent, you can now specify these extended options:

```python
setup(
    # ... other setup parameters
    libraries=[
        (
            "my_clib",
            {
                "sources": ["src/foo.c", "src/bar.c"],
                "obj_deps": {
                    "src/foo.c": ["include/foo.h", "config.h"],
                    "src/bar.c": ["include/bar.h", "config.h"],
                    "": ["common.h"] # Global dependency
                },
                "cflags": ["-O3", "-Wall"],
                "include_dirs": ["include"],
            }
        )
    ],
    # ...
)
```

In this example:
-   `"src/foo.c"` depends on `"include/foo.h"`, `"config.h"`, and the global dependency `"common.h"`.
-   `"src/bar.c"` depends on `"include/bar.h"`, `"config.h"`, and the global dependency `"common.h"`.
-   The compiler will be invoked with `-O3` and `-Wall` flags, and the `include` directory will be added to the include path.
